title: rubus podophyllos subsp. erythrostylus
intro: Rubus podophyllos subsp. erythrostylus, also known as Himalayan raspberry or Indian raspberry, is a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height and produces white flowers followed by red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus podophyllos subsp. erythrostylus include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting immune function, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or tinctures for consumption.
